These are divine names, titles, and eternal/hosts vocabulary added as theological frame references — not direct inner-being terms. registry.description: Peace is the condition of wholeness, right relationship, and rest — the Hebrew shalom covering far more than the absence of conflict. It means completeness, prosperity, welfare, the flourishing of everything and everyone in their proper place. It is used of the end of hostilities, of the quality of a life well-lived, of the relationship between a person and God when that relationship is restored. The New Testament declares that Christ himself is our peace: through him the fundamental rupture between humanity and God is healed. Peace is the signature of the kingdom.

terms.medium_def:
The Deity; 
called LORD or LORD (KJV= Jehovah) (יהוה); or LORD (KJV= Jehovah) (יְהֹוִה); or LORD (יָהּ); or Lord (אֲדֹנָי); or God (KJV= Elohim) (אֱלֹהִים); or Baal (KJV= Baali; NIV= Master) (בַּעֲלִי); or Almighty (שַׁדַּי); or Most High (עַל); or Most High (Aramiac עִלַּי); or Most High (KJV= "most High") or Most High (KJV= Highest) (עֶלְיוֹן); or Most High (Aramiac עֶלְיוֹן); or God/ Most High (KJV= "most high God") (אֵל עֶלְיוֹן); or God (אֵל=God); or El/-Elohe (אֵל אֱלֹהִים); or El/-Elohe Israel (=Alt tag) (אֵל אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל); or Banner (KJV= Nissi) (נֵס); or Banner (KJV= Nissi) (=Alt tag) (יְהֹוָה נִסִּי); or Peace (KJV= Shalom) (שָׁלוֹם); or Peace (KJV= Shalom) (=Alt tag) (יְהֹוָה שָׁלוֹם); or Provider (KJV= Jireh) (יִרְאֶה); or Provider (KJV= Jireh) (=Alt tag) (יִרְאֶה); or LORD our Righteousness (NIV= Righteous Saviour) (צִדְקֵנוּ); or LORD our Righteousness (NIV= Righteous Saviour) (יְהֹוָה צִדְקֵנוּ (=Alt tag)); or Everlasting (NIV= Eternal) (עוֹלָם); or Jealous (קַנָּא); or Hosts (NIV= Almighty) (צָבָא); or Highest (ὕψιστος); or "hosts" (KJV= "sabaoth;" NIV= Almighty) (σαβαώθ); or Abba (ἀββά); or Eli (ἠλί); or Eloi (ἐλωΐ) §This name means completeness, peace
